subject: ap-calculus-bc
theme: Integration measures accumulated quantities — areas between curves, volumes of solids, displacement, and (BC-only) arc length of curves.

core_concepts.title: Average Value of a Function
core_concepts.detail: The average value of $f$ on $[a,b]$ is $f_{\text{avg}} = \frac{1}{b-a}\int_a^b f(x)\,dx$. By MVT for integrals, there exists a $c$ where $f(c) = f_{\text{avg}}$.

core_concepts.title: Displacement vs. Total Distance
core_concepts.detail: Displacement is $\int_a^b v(t)\,dt$ (net change, can be negative). Total distance is $\int_a^b |v(t)|\,dt$ (always non-negative). Split at zeros of $v(t)$ to compute total distance.

core_concepts.title: Area Between Curves
core_concepts.detail: Area $= \int_a^b [f(x) - g(x)]\,dx$ where $f(x) \geq g(x)$. When curves cross, split into subintervals. Can also integrate with respect to $y$.

core_concepts.title: Volumes: Disc and Washer
core_concepts.detail: Disc: $V = \pi\int_a^b [f(x)]^2\,dx$ (solid, no hole). Washer: $V = \pi\int_a^b ([R(x)]^2 - [r(x)]^2)\,dx$ (outer minus inner radius squared).

core_concepts.title: Volumes: Known Cross Sections
core_concepts.detail: $V = \int_a^b A(x)\,dx$ where $A(x)$ is the cross-sectional area. Common shapes: squares ($s^2$), semicircles ($\frac{\pi}{8}d^2$), equilateral triangles ($\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}s^2$).

core_concepts.title: Arc Length (BC)
core_concepts.detail: $L = \int_a^b \sqrt{1 + [f'(x)]^2}\,dx$ gives the length of the curve $y = f(x)$ from $x = a$ to $x = b$. This is a BC-only topic.

formulas.name: Average Value
formulas.description: The height of a rectangle with the same base and same area as the region under f. Divides the total accumulation by the interval length.
formulas.example: Average value of $f(x) = x^2$ on $[0, 3]$: $\frac{1}{3}\int_0^3 x^2\,dx = \frac{1}{3} \cdot 9 = 3$.

formulas.name: Volume by Disc Method
formulas.description: Revolve y = f(x) around the x-axis. Each slice is a circle with radius f(x). Integrate the area π[f(x)]² across the interval.
formulas.example: Revolve $y = \sqrt{x}$ around the $x$-axis from 0 to 4: $V = \pi\int_0^4 x\,dx = \pi \cdot 8 = 8\pi$.

formulas.name: Volume by Washer Method
formulas.description: Revolve a region between two curves. R(x) is the outer radius, r(x) is the inner radius. The integral computes the area of each washer.
formulas.example: Revolve region between $y = x$ and $y = x^2$ around $x$-axis on $[0,1]$: $V = \pi\int_0^1(x^2 - x^4)\,dx = \pi\left[\frac{x^3}{3} - \frac{x^5}{5}\right]_0^1 = \frac{2\pi}{15}$.

formulas.name: Arc Length
formulas.description: BC-only. Measures the actual length of a curve, not just horizontal distance. Derived from the Pythagorean theorem on infinitesimal pieces of the curve.

exam_tip: For washer problems, sketch the region and identify which curve is farther from the axis of rotation (outer radius $R$) vs. closer (inner radius $r$). The formula is $\pi\int(R^2 - r^2)$, not $\pi\int(R - r)^2$.
